steps.title: Identify the passage’s central focus
steps.content: The passage opens with Teresa startled awake by a news report, unsure when she fell asleep. It then draws a deliberate contrast: on most nights sleep came “quickly” and “violently,” but last night she waded into it “slowly, almost willingly” — like moonlight inviting you into a receding tide. The passage is built around Teresa’s awakening and that contrast in how she fell asleep.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Check each choice against the passage’s actual content:
  • Choice A — ‘How Teresa has awakened and how she fell asleep earlier’: Matches both elements — her startled awakening by the news report and the extended contrast between her usual violent sleep and last night’s slow, willing drift. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Why Teresa only watches television news before going to bed’: The television news appears only as the trigger for her awakening; the passage never explains why she watches it or suggests it is a habit. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘How Teresa is enjoying the change in the weather’: The clouds are a brief atmospheric detail in the opening line; Teresa’s enjoyment of weather is never the subject. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘What Teresa expects to do the following day’: The passage contains no mention of Teresa’s plans or expectations for the next day. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
